pending.body: In `safe_plan_hash_backfill` (fs.rs:2628-2649), when the entry was created BEFORE any plan was persisted, both `plan_hash` and `snapshot_plan_hash_at_creation` are `None`. If a plan is later persisted and then the entry is revisited (via `update_task_run` or re-`record_task_run_start`), the backfill correctly refuses because `snapshot_plan_hash_at_creation` is `None` (legacy guard). However, this means entries created in a no-plan-yet state can NEVER be auto-populated from the snapshot, even though the system now knows which plan is executing. The acceptance criterion says 'Lineage entries auto-populate plan_hash from the milestone snapshot when callers omit it' — this edge case (start before plan, complete after plan) is not covered. Consider: when `snapshot_plan_hash_at_creation` is `None` AND `plan_hash` is `None` AND the entry is still Running (not terminal), it is safe to populate both `plan_hash` and `snapshot_plan_hash_at_creation` from the current snapshot during revisitation, since no prior plan version existed to conflict with.

src/adapters/fs.rs:3002-3030 finds an existing running attempt via the collapsed canonical view, but then repairs only the first raw row satisfying same_attempt. In duplicate-row states, that first raw row can be the stale copy with plan_hash=None while another duplicate already has the populated plan_hash/snapshot_plan_hash_at_creation. Replaying record_bead_start with plan_hash omitted then returns and writes the stale row unchanged, so the lineage file and BeadStarted journal never get repaired even though the canonical attempt already had the missing metadata. Repair from the merged canonical entry, or pick the most complete duplicate, before deciding there is no change.

src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:320-337 does not merge snapshot_plan_hash_at_creation, even though duplicate cleanup in src/adapters/fs.rs:3308-3315 and canonical collapsing in src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:630-644 both depend on merge_attempt_entries. If the surviving primary row is the stale duplicate and the only provenance-bearing copy is in a duplicate being removed, the merge drops that provenance permanently. After that, safe_plan_hash_backfill (src/adapters/fs.rs:2628-2648) can never recover plan_hash for that attempt, so the new issue-3 repair silently stops working on duplicate/replay paths.

run_snapshot.status_summary: running: final review restart round 12 -> Plan and Implement
